name: ECANet-New Dataset
description: Elementary Cellular Automata (ECA) dataset for rule inference under synchronous and asynchronous update regimes. Generated for training and evaluating ECANet-New models.

phase1.description: Synchronous update only (alpha=1.0). Clean rule inference baseline with no stochastic noise.

phase2.description: Asynchronous update, stratified by alpha. Every rule appears at every alpha level exactly K times. Guaranteed balanced coverage with no random imbalance.

field_descriptions.orbits: Raw ECA orbit over T=100 timesteps on a W=20 grid. Binary values {0, 1}.
field_descriptions.rule_bits: 8-bit binary representation of the ECA rule number (LSB first, bits 0-7).
field_descriptions.alphas: Alpha value used during simulation. alpha=1.0 means fully synchronous; lower values introduce stochastic asynchrony.
field_descriptions.rule_ids: Integer rule number (0-255) corresponding to each sample.

file_format.storage_order: Rule-wise within each alpha group. All samples for rule 0 first, then rule 1, etc. Use DataLoader shuffle=True during training.

loading_example.python:
import numpy as np
orbits    = np.load('ECA_Data_New/phase1/train/orbits.npy')
rule_bits = np.load('ECA_Data_New/phase1/train/rule_bits.npy')
alphas    = np.load('ECA_Data_New/phase1/train/alphas.npy')
rule_ids  = np.load('ECA_Data_New/phase1/train/rule_ids.npy')

task.target: 8-bit rule representation (rule_bits) or integer rule ID (rule_ids)
task.auxiliary_target: alpha value per sample (for phase2 alpha-conditioned models)
